front: Describe the tryptophan-kynurenine pathway and its neurological implications.
back: Tryptophan is metabolized via IDO/TDO to kynurenine, which branches to neuroprotective kynurenic acid or neurotoxic quinolinic acid. Imbalance occurs in depression, schizophrenia, and neurodegenerative diseases. IDO activation by inflammation depletes tryptophan.
